children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: A duty assignment for an active duty member of the Coast Guard in support of a declaration of a major disaster or emergency by the President under the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act ( et seq.) or in response to a spill of national significance shall be treated, for the purpose of , as a duty assignment in support of a contingency operation.42 U.S.C. 5121section 701(e) of title 10

children.heading: .—Spill of national significance
children.label: 1 .—Spill of national significance
children.text: The term “spill of national significance” means a discharge of oil or a hazardous substance that is declared by the Commandant to be a spill of national significance.

children.heading: .—Discharge
children.label: 2 .—Discharge
children.text: The term “discharge” has the meaning given that term in section 1001 of the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 ().33 U.S.C. 2701
